data.headline: Serving Connecticut

data.title: Where We Work

counties.name: Fairfield County
counties.image_alt: Fairfield County, Connecticut — BuiltWell CT home remodeling service area
counties.description: Serving all of Fairfield County with dedicated local crews. From Greenwich estates to Ridgefield colonials, we know the housing stock and building departments across the county.

counties.name: New Haven County
counties.cta_label: Learn more about New Haven County
counties.image_alt: New Haven County, Connecticut — BuiltWell CT home remodeling service area
counties.description: Served from our Orange, CT office. We cover every town in New Haven County from coastal Branford and Madison to inland Woodbridge and Cheshire, delivering expert remodeling across the region.

data.subtitle: We maintain dedicated teams in both Fairfield and New Haven Counties so every project gets local attention, fast response times, and familiarity with your town's building department.

data.title: Full-Service Home Remodeling Across Both Counties
data.subtitle: Every service is available in both Fairfield County and New Haven County. Same team, same standards, same CT HIC license #0668405.

data.title: Headquartered in Orange, Serving Connecticut
data.content:
BuiltWell CT is headquartered in Orange, Connecticut, centrally located between our two service regions. Our New Haven County team operates directly from this office, while our Fairfield County team works from within the county to minimize drive times and maximize responsiveness.

We hold Connecticut Home Improvement Contractor license #0668405 and carry full liability and workers' compensation insurance. Every project is managed by our in-house team, never subcontracted out. Whether your home is in Greenwich or Guilford, you get the same project management structure, the same communication standards, and the same commitment to quality craftsmanship.

Permitting requirements vary significantly by municipality across both counties. Some towns require separate electrical, plumbing, and building permits. Others consolidate under a single application. Towns like Greenwich, New Canaan, Ridgefield, and Guilford have historic district requirements that add a design review step. We handle all permit applications and inspection coordination regardless of which town your home is in.

Connecticut's housing stock presents unique challenges that general contractors from out of state regularly underestimate. Pre-1920s homes in towns like New Haven, Guilford, and Greenwich feature balloon framing, stone foundations, and plaster walls that require specialized knowledge. Coastal towns from Westport to Madison deal with elevated humidity, salt air exposure, and higher water tables near Long Island Sound. Much of Fairfield County sits on bedrock with shallow water tables, creating hydrostatic pressure on foundations. We understand these conditions because we have been working in them for over 15 years.
